chapter_title.en: Chapter 18 – Buying real estate and selling it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/42)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/42)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) لِمُصَادِفٍ مَوْلَاهُ اتَّخِذْ عُقْدَةً أَوْ ضَيْعَةً فَإِنَّ الرَّجُلَ إِذَا نَزَلَتْ بِهِ النَّازِلَةُ أَوِ الْمُصِيبَةُ فَذَكَرَ أَنَّ وَرَاءَ ظَهْرِهِ مَا يُقِيمُ عِيَالَهُ كَانَ أَسْخَى لِنَفْسِهِ .

en.hubeali: Al Hassan Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad Al nahdy, from Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, from Muhammad Bin Murazim, from his father who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said to Musadif, a slave of his<sup>asws</sup>: ‘Take real estate or a place (house etc.), for the man, when there descends unto him an affliction or the difficulty, so he remembers that behind his back is what his family can stand upon, he would be more generous with himself’.
en.sarwar: 5. Al-Hassan ibn Muhammad has narrated from Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Nahdiy from Ya’qub ibn Yazid from Muhammad ibn Murazim from his father who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, Alayhi al-Salam, once said to his Mawla’, Musadif, ‘Find some real property or goods; when man faces hardship or misfortune, and then finds out that behind him there is something for support of his family, he feels more generous to allow his soul to depart (his body).”’
